message:
refactor(fabric): extract certified request helpers, use struct-based decision evaluation

Decompose the monolithic request_certified_with_obligation method and
evaluate_fabric_decision function into smaller, testable pieces:

Decision evaluation:
- New FabricDecisionEvaluationInput struct replaces 5 loose parameters
  (kind, cell_id, subject, delivery_class, annotations) with a single
  named struct.
- evaluate_fabric_decision now takes the input struct, and borrows
  posterior/ctx instead of consuming them, avoiding unnecessary clones
  at each call site.
- All 4 decision types (Routing, Retry, Capability, DeliveryClassEscalation)
  updated to construct FabricDecisionEvaluationInput and bind posterior
  before the call.

Certified request decomposition:
- validate_certified_request_admission: validates certificate, checks
  delivery class is ObligationBacked+, verifies certificate/admission
  field consistency.
- build_certified_request_capabilities: constructs publish and subscribe
  FabricCapabilities from a subject pattern.
- enforce_certified_request_capabilities: enforces both capabilities,
  returning the first error if any.
- release_certified_publish_reservation: releases a prepared publish
  reservation on error paths.
- CertifiedReplyBuild struct + build_certified_reply_artifacts: encapsulate
  obligation commit, reply certificate creation, and delivery receipt
  generation.
- apply_prepared_publish inline cleanup: remove redundant mut transition
  variable by using a let-binding block.

No behavioral changes — all paths produce identical results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
